'''Trevura''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]]-controlled [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] with a prominent [[Droid/Legends|droid]] population. At some point between [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] and [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], an [[Trevura droid resistance|underground droid resistance group]] on Trevura hired the [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] [[S-R6]] to [[Death/Legends|kill]] the local [[Governor/Legends|governor]], [[Merno Blask]]. In response, Blask arranged for a recently developed anti-droid [[inhibit setting]] for [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] to be field-tested in [[Voma]], the planet's [[Capital/Legends|capital]].

During that [[Time/Legends|time]], a group of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] agents, seeking to escort a vital [[Unidentified astromech droid (Trevura)|astromech droid]] off-planet, killed a pair of Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] equipped with the experimental inhibitor [[DLT-19 heavy blaster rifle/Legends|guns]]. Shortly afterward, S-R6 established contact with the Rebels and met them at [[Essar's garage|his garage]], whereupon stormtrooper reinforcements engaged everyone present at the structure in a fierce [[Lightfight|firefight]].

==Description==
Trevura was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim's]]<ref name="Wizards" /> [[Trevura system/Legends|Trevura system]].<ref name="EAOC" />

==History==
===Threatened by droids===
[[File:StormtrooperGunTrevura.jpg|thumb|right|200px|On Trevura, Imperial stormtroopers field-tested experimental blaster rifles.]]
At some point between [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] and [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]],<ref name="Date">"[[Gun Nut]]" is set in the [[Rebellion era]], which {{Blog|holocron|24|Star Wars Eras}} dates to between [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] and [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]].</ref> an [[Trevura droid resistance|underground droid enclave]] on Trevura hired the off-world [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] [[S-R6]] to [[Death/Legends|kill]] the local [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Governor/Legends|governor]], [[Merno Blask]]. When Blask learned that an [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] would make an attempt on his life, he arranged for an Imperial research-and-development team to carry out a field test of the [[inhibit setting]], a recently developed [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] mode of operation designed to disable droids, in Trevura's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]], [[Voma]].<ref name="Wizards" />

As part of the test, Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] were equipped with [[DLT-19 heavy blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]] outfitted with the inhibitor setting. The stormtroopers then patrolled the city's streets, [[Ion blaster/Legends|ionizing]] any nearby droids under the pretense of the latter having gone rogue. The [[Trooper/Legends|troopers']] actions were discreetly observed by a small group of Imperial [[Technician/Legends|technicians]] who were taking notes on the performance of the experimental [[Blaster rifle/Legends|weapons]]. In addition, S-R6, an avid collector of various firearms whose interest had been drawn by the appearance of the modified [[Rifle/Legends|rifles]], utilized a [[Repulsorlift/Legends|hovering]] [[Espionage droid|spy eye droid]] to monitor the Imperial activities.<ref name="Wizards" />

===The "inhibitor incident"===
{{Main|"Inhibitor incident"}}
{{RPG|"[[Gun Nut]]"}}
At the same [[Time/Legends|time]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] dispatched a group of individuals to Trevura. The Rebel agents were to safely escort an [[Unidentified astromech droid (Trevura)|astromech droid]] carrying sensitive information about Imperial weapons research off the planet, under the cover of assessing local land and considering its purchase. In Voma, a pair of stormtroopers attempted to disable and search the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]], leading to a short [["Inhibitor incident"|firefight]] in which the group killed the two troopers.<ref name="Wizards" />

Shortly thereafter, the Rebel agents came into contact with S-R6, who was masquerading as a simplistic [[ASP-series labor droid/Legends|ASP-series labor droid]], and visited the assassin's [[Essar's garage|garage]], where the droid expressed his eager interest in the inhibitor guns. At that moment, a group of stormtroopers summoned by the Imperial technicians as reinforcements attacked the structure. After an intense [[Lightfight|firefight]], the Rebel group, assisted by S-R6 and his [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]], [[T-3PO (S-R6)|T-3PO]], emerged victorious. S-R6 then escaped with the inhibitor guns while the Rebel agents expeditiously took the astromech droid off-planet aboard their [[Starship/Legends|starship]].<ref name="Wizards" />
{{Endgame}}

==Inhabitants==
Trevura was an Imperial world that was considered to be relatively civilized. It was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to a sizable population of droids, which were intensively [[Merchant/Legends|traded]] on the planet. Due to the local governor's strong [[Anti-droid sentiment/Legends|anti-droid prejudices]], droids on Trevura were frequently harassed by the local [[Trevura law enforcement|law enforcement]].<ref name="Wizards" />

==Locations==
{{Main|Voma}}
Voma was the capital city of Trevura. Droids could be seen in the city's streets. Several blocks away from where the Rebel agents confronted Imperial stormtroopers, a garage used by the assassin droid S-R6 lay adjacent to an urban alley.<ref name="Wizards" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Pardon my intrusion, sirs. I am T-3P0 ''[sic]'', a translator droid. My master couldn't help but notice that you have acquired some unusual ordnance.|T-3PO approaches the player characters in one of the alternate scenarios in "Gun Nut"|<ref name="Wizards" />}}
Trevura was created for "[[Gun Nut]]," a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] mini-adventure for the [[Wizards of the Coast]] ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' written by [[Jeff Quick]] and published on [[Wizards.com]] on [[April 11]], [[2002]].<ref name="Wizards" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Trevura [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Trevura itself, in grid square K-14.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

"Gun Nut" presents two alternate scenarios of events that depend on the player characters' actions concerning the inhibitor guns of the stormtroopers they have killed in the streets of Voma. If the characters pick up the weapons, they are soon approached by S-R6's protocol droid, T-3PO. The droid offers to buy the firearms on the behalf of his master, and if the characters agree, leads them to S-R6's garage, where the assassin droid personally continues the negotiation for the weapons.<ref name="Wizards" />

If the characters do not pick up the inhibitor guns, they are then collected by S-R6 in the disguise of a [[Labor droid/Legends|labor droid]] performing cleanup on behalf of the [[Trevura government]]. The "labor droid" then beckons for the characters to follow to his garage, upon which they can either choose to ignore the droid and quickly make preparations to leave the planet or follow him to his hideout, where S-R6 proceeds to question the characters about their experience with the inhibitor guns. Regardless of which scenario the player characters follow, they end up in S-R6's garage, proceeding to the remaining part of the adventure.<ref name="Wizards" />
